content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 27, 1990


Roberta L. Weintraub
Board of Education
City of Los Angeles
P.O. Box 3307
Los Angeles, CA  90051

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-210

Dear Ms. Weintraub:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice regarding your responsibilities under the new "personal use" provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

     May campaign funds be used to pay for your travel to and tuition for an educational program for senior state and local government executives focusing on improving administrative skills?

CONCLUSION

     Expenditures made for a program for public officials intended to improve their administrative skills and to provide training to be used in the performance of the official's governmental duties are directly related to a political or governmental purpose. Consequently, campaign funds may be used to pay to attend the program.

FACTS

     You have asked whether you may use campaign funds to pay for travel and tuition (which includes accommodations) to a summer program offered by Harvard University entitled "The Program for Senior Executives in State and Local Government" (the "program"). The program is for senior executives in state and local government.


_________________________

1/     Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

File No. A-90-210
Page 2

and their elected counterparts and runs three weeks. The program
is designed to help participants sharpen their problem solving
skills, gain insight into social and economic forces that shape
their operational environment, examine their ethical and professional responsibilities, understand the role of public executives
in improving productivity and managing human resources. Tuition
for the program is $6,800.

ANALYSIS

Effective January 1, 1990, the Act was amended by Senate Bill
1431 to include new provisions that regulate the appropriate use
of campaign funds. (Section 85800, et seq., the "personal use"
law.) The purpose of the legislation was "to ensure that
candidates, elected officers and the people clearly recognize acceptable and unacceptable uses of campaign funds." (Senate Elections Committee Analysis of SB 1431.)

The general rule of the new personal use law is that any
expenditure of campaign funds must be, at a minimum, personally
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
(Section 85801.) However, under some circumstances the personal
use law requires a stricter standard. For example, where an
expenditure confers a substantial personal benefit on the
candidate or officeholder, the expenditure must be directly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.2/
(Section 85801.)

Tuition

Clearly, where campaign funds are used to pay a fee for your
attendance at an educational program, you have received a personal
benefit equivalent to the payment. According to the facts you
provided, the tuition for the program is greater than $100.
Consequently, for the expenditure of campaign funds to be permissible there must be a direct relationship between the expenditure
and a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section
85801.)

Since the program at Harvard is clearly offered for public
officials, and the training provided to the participants will be
used in the performance of their governmental duties, we believe
there is a direct relationship between the expenditure of campaign
funds for the program and a political or governmental purpose.
Consequently, campaign funds may be used to pay tuition for the
program.

_____________________________

2/ A substantial personal benefit is defined as a direct personal
benefit to the candidate or officeholder of $100 or more. (Section 85800(b)(2).)

File No. A-90-210
Page 3

Travel To and From the Program

Section 85802(a) provides:

Campaign funds shall not be used to pay or
reimburse the candidate, the elected officer, or
employees or staff of the campaign committee or the
elected officer's governmental agency for travel
expenses and necessary accommodations except when these
expenditures are directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose.

(Emphasis added.)

Therefore, the use of campaign funds to pay for transporta-
tion to and from the site of the program is permissible, provided
the program is directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose. Since we concluded above that the program
is directly related to a political or governmental purpose associ-
ated with your duties as a board member for the Los Angeles Uni-
fied School District, payment for travel to and from the program
is also permissible.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
